mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through reduced aggregate demand and profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2009 payroll employment at 131.5M, down from 137M in 2008
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative PAYEMS momentum does not produce significantly lower 20-day returns than positive momentum periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation and forward guidance credibility, anchoring long-term discount rates low and supporting equity valuations. This always-on signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as liquidity effects persist.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals strengthening consumer demand and forward earnings growth expectations. This threshold-triggered signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums compress in response to improved demand visibility.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 339.99B in May 2009, recovering from 2008 lows
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 when RSAFS growth > 1% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal supply-side capacity utilization and labor productivity. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, it indicates sustainable profit margin expansion and positive forward returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures non-linear supply-demand balance effects.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.07 in April 2009, PAYEMS at 132.15M in May 2009
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 58% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 500K units signals deteriorating real estate demand and reduced construction employment multiplier effects. This threshold-triggered signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings guidance is revised downward by cyclical sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 458K in April 2009, well below pre-crisis levels
regime_hint: housing market depression and credit constraint regime
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 when HOUST < 500K over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth below 2% combined with FEDFUNDS near zero signals deflationary risk and monetary policy credibility. This interaction-driven signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real discount rates remain suppressed and equity risk premiums compress.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 212.67 in April 2009, FEDFUNDS at 0.18% in May 2009
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 60% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 9.0% signals severe labor market slack and reduced consumer confidence. In this regime, equity valuations compress due to earnings downgrades and elevated recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism is regime-conditional: it is strongest in post-shock recovery phases.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.4% in May 2009, near peak of financial crisis
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE > 9%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in high-unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter shows positive acceleration when the economy exits recession. This always-on trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations improve and equity risk premiums normalize from crisis levels.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11353.7B in Q1 2009, beginning recovery from 2008 contraction
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 58% of periods with positive GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
